questions.text: What historical event does 'Al-Ahzab' refer to?
questions.answer: The Battle of the Trench (Khandaq), where various tribes combined forces to besiege Madinah.

questions.text: How did Allah help the believers during the Battle of the Trench?
questions.answer: He sent against the enemies a wind and hosts (angels) that the people could not see.

questions.text: How is the Prophet's relationship with the believers described in Verse 6?
questions.answer: The Prophet is closer to the believers than their own selves, and his wives are their mothers.

questions.text: What is the ruling on 'Zihar' (calling one's wife 'mother') mentioned in Verse 4?
questions.answer: Allah has not made your wives whom you declare as your mothers your actual mothers; it is just a word from your mouths.

questions.text: What is the command regarding the naming of adopted children?
questions.answer: Call them by [the names of] their fathers; it is more just in the sight of Allah.

questions.text: Who is the 'Excellent Example' (Uswatun Hasanah) for the believers?
questions.answer: Prophet Muhammad (SAW).

questions.text: What was the reaction of the believers when they saw the combined armies (Ahzab)?
questions.answer: They said 'This is what Allah and His Messenger had promised us...'; it only increased them in faith and submission.

questions.text: How did the hypocrites react during the siege of Madinah?
questions.answer: They said 'Allah and His Messenger did not promise us except delusion' and sought permission to leave, saying their houses were exposed.

questions.text: What choice did the Prophet (SAW) give to his wives in Verses 28-29?
questions.answer: To choose between the life of this world and its adornment (and be released kindly) or Allah, His Messenger, and the Home of the Hereafter.

questions.text: What is the status of the 'Mothers of the Believers' (the Prophet's wives) compared to other women?
questions.answer: They are not like any other women; if they fear Allah, they should not be soft in speech [to men] and should stay in their houses.

questions.text: What is the 'Verse of Purification' (Ayat al-Tathir)?
questions.answer: Verse 33: 'Allah intends only to remove from you the impurity [of sin], O people of the [Prophet's] household, and to purify you with [thorough] purification'.

questions.text: What is the 'Seal of the Prophets' (Khatam an-Nabiyyin)?
questions.answer: Prophet Muhammad (SAW); Verse 40 states he is not the father of any of your men, but the Messenger of Allah and the last of the Prophets.

questions.text: What is the command to the believers regarding sending blessings on the Prophet?
questions.answer: Allah and His angels send blessings upon the Prophet, so 'O you who have believed, ask [Allah to confer] blessing upon him and ask [Allah to grant him] peace'.

questions.text: What is the etiquette for entering the Prophet's house as a guest?
questions.answer: Do not enter unless invited for a meal, do not wait for its preparation, and when finished, disperse without staying for conversation.

questions.text: What is the command regarding 'Hijab' for the Prophet's wives, daughters, and believing women?
questions.answer: To bring down over themselves part of their outer garments (Jalabib); that is more suitable so they will be known and not abused.

questions.text: What is the 'Trust' (Amanah) mentioned at the end of the Surah?
questions.answer: The trust of moral responsibility and obedience which the heavens, earth, and mountains refused to carry out of fear, but man undertook it.

questions.text: How is man described in his undertaking of the Amanah?
questions.answer: As 'unjust and ignorant' (Zaluman Jahula) to himself for the weight of the responsibility he took.

questions.text: What should believers say to have their deeds amended and sins forgiven?
questions.answer: They should fear Allah and speak a word of appropriate justice (Qawlan Sadida).

questions.text: Who was the companion mentioned by name in this Surah?
questions.answer: Zayd (bin Harithah) (RA), in the context of his divorce and the Prophet's subsequent marriage to Zaynab bint Jahsh.

questions.text: What is the consequence of hurting the Messenger of Allah?
questions.answer: Allah has cursed them in this world and the Hereafter and prepared for them a humiliating punishment.
